violations.description: Dapr fixture sleeps 2 seconds after health before re-checking the app.
violations.suggestion: Replace the fixed warmup with bounded polling of the actor/metadata condition that must become true.

violations.description: Nine EventStore integration test classes each use IClassFixture<JwtAuthenticatedWebApplicationFactory>.
violations.suggestion: Consider a collection fixture for the shared JWT-authenticated factory, keeping specialized rate-limiting factories separate.

violations.description: Thread.Sleep in file retry loops blocks the runner thread.
violations.suggestion: Use async retry waits with the test cancellation token.

recommendations: Keep the new FakeTimeProvider collector approach; it removed the prior 51-second hot spot.
Replace fixed Dapr post-health delay with readiness polling.
Reduce repeated WebApplicationFactory startups where fixtures are read-only and safe to share.
summary: Performance is materially better than the May 4 review because DaprHealthHistoryCollectorTests no longer wait on wall clock. Remaining cost is mostly fixture startup duplication and fixed warmups.
